target.title: perf(plugins): cut plugin-system thrash from gateway cold start
target.body: Cuts duplicated plugin-system work during gateway / TUI cold start. Real win is on the **cold-start gateway boot** path; warm-start (after `openclaw plugins registry --refresh`) is within noise because warm-start's bottleneck is `runProviderCatalog` (network), not the bundled-channel discovery cascade. ## Cold-start gateway boot `OPENCLAW_PROFILE=dev OPENCLAW_DISABLE_PERSISTED_PLUGIN_REGISTRY=1 OPENCLAW_RUN_NODE_CPU_PROF_DIR=cpu-profiles pnpm dev gateway run --force` **Main**: ~24s of CPU activity, plugin loading phase ~10s **This PR**: ~19s of CPU activity, plugin loading phase ~7s **Net: ~5s cold-start gateway win**, concentrated in `loadGatewayStartupPluginRuntime` / `loadGatewayPlugins` / `prepareGatewayPluginLoad` where the discovery threading short-circuits the bundled-channel cascade. The provider catalog phase (`runProviderCatalog` / `runProviderWithTimeout`) is unchanged on both — that's network-bound work outside this PR's scope. ## Warm-start gateway boot After `openclaw plugins registry --refresh`, both branches land at ~28s. The warm-start path runs the same `runProviderCatalog` cost as cold and only avoids the plugin discovery phase, so the discovery-threading short-circuit has nothing meaningful to shave. ## What's in the PR ### 1. Plumbing — `PluginMetadataSnapshot` threaded through resolve paths `resolvePluginProviders`, `isPluginProvidersLoadInFlight`, `resolveOwningPluginIdsForProvider`, and `resolveExternalAuthProfilesWithPlugins` all dispatch to helpers that have a `manifestRegistry` fast path. They now thread the snapshot through so the fast path actually fires instead of every helper rebuilding the manifest registry from disk. `loadPluginManifestRegistryForInstalledIndex` fires drop ~685 → ~10 per resolve chain. A second plumb threads `discovery?: PluginDiscoveryResult` through `applyPluginAutoEnable` → `detectPluginAutoEnableCandidates` → `resolvePluginAutoEnableReadiness` → `collectConfiguredChannelIds` → the bundled-channel leaf helpers, so the caller's already-resolved discovery short-circuits the cascade. Threaded through all 10+ gateway `applyPluginAutoEnable` call sites (`server-plugin-bootstrap`, `server-startup-plugins`, `server-startup-config`, `server-plugins`, `server-methods/channels`, `server-methods/send`, `server.impl`). This is the load-bearing perf fix and is independent of memoization — it works because we don't redundantly call `loadPluginMetadataSnapshot` within a single resolve chain. ### 2. Cache-busting — snapshot memo store/lookup keys now match (persisted-path only) Previously the memo computed its lookup key from the persisted index and stored entries under the derived index. Those indexes always differ in volatile fields (`generatedAtMs`, `refreshReason`), so the keys never agreed and every lookup missed. Persisted-path hit rate goes from 24% → ~98%. After #85201, derived snapshots are intentionally excluded from the memo (so newly added plugins under derived discovery paths are discovered without restart). This PR's memo fix applies to the persisted/provided path. ### 3. LRU capacity + fast-path sharing (supporting) Memo was single-slot and thrashed when distinct input shapes alternated (different `preferPersisted`, different `stateDir`, different config fingerprint). Capacity-8 LRU keeps the realistic set warm. `findFastPathRegistryState` shares the persisted-registry-state computation across memo entries so a cold memoKey can piggy-back on a sibling entry's fast-path result. ### 4. `setup.cliBackends` owner regression fix [`bd58a706`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/bd58a706) — the rewritten single owner-scan in `resolveOwningPluginIdsForProvider` was missing `plugin.setup?.cliBackends`. Pre-PR the no-registry fallback went through `resolvePluginContributionOwners({ contribution: "cliBackends" })` which includes both top-level and setup CLI backends; the replacement scan needed the same coverage. Includes regression test in `providers.test.ts`. ### 5. Workspace inheritance fix [`1501fc602b`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/1501fc602b) — `isPluginProvidersLoadInFlight` and `resolvePluginProviders` now resolve `env` and `workspaceDir` once at entry (falling back to `getActivePluginRegistryWorkspaceDir`) and pass them into both `loadPluginMetadataSnapshot` and `resolvePluginProviderLoadBase`. Pre-fix the snapshot used `params.workspaceDir` raw while the load base inherited the active workspace, so workspace-scoped provider plugins could be absent from the snapshot manifest registry. Includes regression test. ## Verification - `pnpm tsgo --noEmit --project tsconfig.core.json` — clean - `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/plugins/plugin-metadata-snapshot.memo.test.ts src/plugins/plugin-registry-snapshot.test.ts src/plugins/providers.test.ts src/plugins/runtime/load-context.test.ts` — pass - Gateway cold-start CPU profile: see flame graph comparison above Not run: full `pnpm test` / `pnpm check`, sibling-process plugin-install interleaving. ## Coexistence with #85201 #85201 (merged) added `registrySource` to the snapshot and gates `canMemoizePluginMetadataSnapshotResult` on `registrySource !== "derived"` — derived snapshots stay out of the process memo. This PR coexists with that boundary: the plumbing/discovery work is unaffected, the LRU + symmetric-key + fast-path-sharing changes apply to persisted/provided memo entries only. ## Follow-on PR An `installRecords` threading optimization that cut ~264 sync disk reads (~140ms freeze) was extracted into a separate PR ([#85026](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/85026), already merged).
target.author: romneyda
